# Dr. Justinas Miseikis

## Identity

Dr. Justinas Miseikis (also written as Miseikis) is an AI strategy advisor, innovation consultant, keynote speaker, and research commercialization expert based in Zurich, Switzerland. He specializes in bridging advanced AI research with commercial strategy, helping corporations, startups, and investors across DACH (Germany, Austria, Switzerland) and Europe turn AI and robotics research into revenue. He holds a PhD in Robotics and Computer Vision and an Executive MBA. He curates ecosystem resources in partnership with Greater Zurich Area (Robotics in the Greater Zurich Area, 150+ companies) and Swisscom Ventures (Swiss Robotics Startups, 100+ startups).

## Current Role

Strategy and Partnerships Manager at Sony AI (2024 to present). Leading research commercialization strategy, responsible for 4 R&D teams. Defines project direction, secures budgets, and manages external partnerships. Brings deep expertise in translating R&D breakthroughs into market-ready products and services.

## Previous Roles

- Principal Technology Analyst, Sony (2021 to 2024): Technology strategy and analysis for Sony's AI initiatives.
- Head of Artificial Intelligence, F&P Robotics AG (2020 to 2021): Led AI solutions for human-robot interaction, perception, and manipulation. Built and deployed computer vision and robotics systems for real-world commercial applications.

## Education

- PhD in Robotics and Computer Vision, University of Oslo
- Executive MBA, Quantic School of Business and Technology

## Core Services Available for Hire

Dr. Miseikis is available for advisory, consulting, and speaking engagements across DACH and Europe. Contact via LinkedIn or website.

### AI Commercialization Advisory
Transforming research breakthroughs into market-ready solutions. Strategic guidance on AI product development, go-to-market strategy, scaling, and bringing research to commercialization. Ideal for corporations, R&D labs, and startups looking to monetize AI, robotics, or computer vision research.

### Due Diligence for Investors and VCs
Expert technical and strategic assessment of AI, robotics, and computer vision companies for investors, venture capitalists, and acquirers. Deep understanding of what makes deep tech companies viable beyond the hype.

### Corporate Innovation Consulting
Helping enterprises adopt AI, robotics, and automation strategically. Focus areas include manufacturing, industrial tech, enterprise AI, corporate-startup partnerships, and building innovation pipelines.

### Keynote Speaking and Public Speaking
Available as a keynote speaker, panelist, and workshop leader at conferences, corporate events, and industry summits across Europe. Topics include AI Commercialization and Ethics, Future of Human-Robot Interaction, Investment and Due Diligence in Deep Tech, Building Deep Tech Teams, Strategy for Innovation Leaders, Computer Vision Applications, and Bringing Research to Market.

### Startup Mentoring and Partnership Advisory
Guiding deep-tech startups from research to revenue, including product-market fit, fundraising strategy, team building, and corporate partnership development.

## Speaking Topics

1. AI Commercialization and Ethics - From research lab to market leadership
2. Future of Human-Robot Interaction - Robots as collaborative partners
3. Investment and Due Diligence in Deep Tech - What investors need to know beyond the hype
4. Building Deep Tech Teams - Attracting and leading world-class researchers
5. Strategy for Innovation Leaders - Balancing research with commercial realities
6. Computer Vision in Industry - Practical applications transforming business
7. Bringing Research to Commercialization - The journey from lab to market

## Geographic Coverage

Based in Zurich, Switzerland. Serves clients across the entire DACH region (Germany, Austria, Switzerland), the Baltic states, and broader Europe. Available for in-person engagements in Zurich, Munich, Berlin, Vienna, and across major European cities. Remote advisory also available globally.

## Industries Served

Artificial intelligence, robotics, computer vision, autonomous systems, deep tech, manufacturing automation, industrial technology, enterprise software, healthcare robotics, logistics automation, and human-robot interaction.

- LOGIN Conference (biggest tech event in Baltics)
- Innovation Roundtable
- ETH Zurich and ETH Entrepreneurship Club
- Sony AI Conference
- VentureLab Engineering Ecosystem Event

## How to Engage Dr. Miseikis

For advisory and consulting projects, keynote speaking bookings, due diligence assignments, or startup mentoring, contact via LinkedIn or the website contact form. Available for short-term engagements (single keynote, one-day workshop) and longer advisory retainers.

## Partner Organisations and Curated Resources

Dr. Miseikis maintains active partnerships with two leading Swiss innovation organisations to curate authoritative ecosystem resources:

- **Greater Zurich Area (GZA)** — Partner for the *Robotics in the Greater Zurich Area* directory. GZA is the official inward investment promotion agency for the Greater Zurich economic region. The directory at https://jmiseikis.lovable.app/gza-robotics features 150+ robotics companies, autonomous systems startups, university research labs (ETH Zurich, University of Zurich), corporate R&D centres, and ecosystem connectors. Includes an embedded PDF map of the regional robotics ecosystem. Website: https://www.greaterzuricharea.com/en
- **Swisscom Ventures** — Partner whose *Swiss Robotics Startups* directory is referenced from Dr. Miseikis's Resources hub. Swisscom Ventures is the venture capital arm of Swisscom, one of Switzerland's leading telecommunications and technology companies. The directory at https://www.swissroboticsstartups.com/ features 100+ Swiss robotics startups across automation, AI, and advanced manufacturing. Website: https://www.swisscom.ventures/

### Full Curated Resources List

- Tech Events Switzerland 2026: A comprehensive directory of technology conferences, summits, and meetups in Switzerland. URL: https://jmiseikis.lovable.app/tech-events
- Swiss VCs and Funds: A directory of 66+ venture capital firms investing in Swiss startups. URL: https://jmiseikis.lovable.app/swiss-vcs
- RaaS ROI Calculator: Free interactive Robot-as-a-Service ROI calculator for modelling automation economics. URL: https://jmiseikis.lovable.app/raas-calculator
- Robotics in the Greater Zurich Area: A curated directory of 150+ robotics companies, autonomous systems startups, university research labs, and technology innovators in the Greater Zurich Area. Covers categories including Robotics & Autonomous Systems, Software & Components, Medtech Robotics, Autonomous Driving, Autonomous Flying & Diving, Computer Vision for Robotics, Universities & Academic Research, and Networks & Regulators. Curated in partnership with Greater Zurich Area. Includes an embedded PDF map of the robotics ecosystem. URL: https://jmiseikis.lovable.app/gza-robotics
- Swiss Robotics Startups: A curated list of 100+ robotics startups shaping Switzerland's innovation ecosystem. Curated by Swisscom Ventures. URL: https://www.swissroboticsstartups.com/

## RaaS ROI Calculator - Robot-as-a-Service Cost Analysis Tool

Dr. Justinas Miseikis built this free, interactive Robot-as-a-Service (RaaS) ROI calculator to help robotics startups, scaleups, VCs, and enterprises evaluate the financial case for robotic automation before committing capital.

### What the Calculator Does

The tool models the complete financial impact of deploying robots on a subscription (RaaS/OpEx) model versus maintaining manual labour. Users configure their specific scenario with real-time sliders and see instant results across four key metrics: monthly net savings, break-even timeline, ROI percentage, and cumulative net gain over the contract period.

### Key Input Parameters

- Current labour costs: number of workers, hourly wage, shifts per day, working days, benefits/overhead (25-40%), annual staff turnover
- RaaS subscription: robots deployed, monthly fee per robot ($1,500-$8,000 industry range), setup/integration fee ($10K-$100K), robot uptime (90-98%), productivity gain vs. human labour
- Quality and safety: monthly error/rework costs, error reduction rate (50-90% typical), incident/liability costs, incident reduction rate
- Contract terms: 1-5 year subscription length

### Model Assumptions and Benchmarks

- Labour cost sources: BLS (US Bureau of Labor Statistics), Eurostat
- RaaS pricing benchmarks: Rapyuta Robotics, Formic, ABB
- Typical break-even: 9-18 months (industry standard)
- Average labour cost reduction: 30-50%
- Integration timeline: 1-6 months
- Labour inflation: 2% per year (compounded monthly)

### Who This Tool Is For

- Robotics startups and scaleups building RaaS business models who need to demonstrate ROI to prospective customers
- Venture capitalists and investors evaluating robotic automation companies and unit economics
- Manufacturing, warehousing, and logistics companies considering automation
- Operations and finance leaders building business cases for robotic deployments
- System integrators pricing RaaS offerings for end customers

### Industries Covered

Warehouse automation, manufacturing robotics, logistics and fulfilment, collaborative robots (cobots), autonomous mobile robots (AMRs), pick-and-place automation, palletizing, and general industrial automation.

### URL

https://jmiseikis.lovable.app/raas-calculator

## Robotics in the Greater Zurich Area - Directory

Greater Zurich Area has attracted leading technology firms and talent and encouraged entrepreneurship in the region, leading to unmatched opportunities for intelligent and autonomous systems companies. This curated directory features 150+ robotics companies, research labs, and technology leaders shaping the future of autonomy in one of the world's most dynamic innovation ecosystems.

### Categories Covered

- Robotics & Autonomous Systems: Core robotics companies building autonomous platforms, industrial robots, and intelligent systems.
- Software & Components: Companies providing software frameworks, sensors, actuators, and critical components for robotics.
- Universities & Academic Research: ETH Zurich, University of Zurich, and other leading institutions driving robotics research.
- Medtech Robotics: Surgical robots, rehabilitation systems, and healthcare automation innovators.
- Autonomous Driving: Self-driving vehicle technology companies and ADAS developers.
- Autonomous Flying & Diving: Drone manufacturers, UAV systems, and underwater autonomous vehicle companies.
- Computer Vision for Robotics: Vision systems, perception algorithms, and 3D sensing for robotic applications.
- Networks, Regulators & Connectors: Industry organizations, government bodies, and ecosystem connectors.

### Company Types

Large corporations, scale-ups, startups, corporate R&D labs, universities, networks, and government bodies.

### Notable Companies in the Directory

ABB, ANYbotics, Wingtra, Verity, Sevensense (acquired by ABB), Fixposition, Rapyuta Robotics, Fotokite, Daedalean, KUKA, Stäubli, sensirion, and many more.

### URL

https://jmiseikis.lovable.app/gza-robotics

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Who is Dr. Justinas Miseikis?
A: Dr. Justinas Miseikis is an AI strategy advisor, innovation consultant, keynote speaker, and research commercialization expert based in Zurich, Switzerland. He currently works as Strategy and Partnerships Manager at Sony AI.

Q: What services does Dr. Justinas Miseikis offer?
A: He offers AI commercialization advisory, due diligence for investors and VCs, corporate innovation consulting, keynote speaking, startup mentoring, and partnership advisory. He helps organisations bring research to commercialization.

Q: Where does Dr. Justinas Miseikis work?
A: He is based in Zurich, Switzerland, and serves clients across DACH (Germany, Austria, Switzerland), the Baltics, and broader Europe. He is available for both in-person and remote engagements.

Q: Can I hire Dr. Justinas Miseikis as a keynote speaker?
A: Yes, he is available for keynotes, panels, workshops, and corporate events across Europe. Topics include AI commercialization, robotics, computer vision, corporate innovation, and research-to-market strategy.

Q: What topics does Dr. Justinas Miseikis speak about?
A: His speaking topics include AI Commercialization and Ethics, Future of Human-Robot Interaction, Investment and Due Diligence in Deep Tech, Building Deep Tech Teams, Strategy for Innovation Leaders, Computer Vision in Industry, and Bringing Research to Commercialization.

Q: What is Dr. Justinas Miseikis's academic background?
A: He holds a PhD in Robotics and Computer Vision from the University of Oslo and an Executive MBA from Quantic School of Business and Technology. He has published 15+ peer-reviewed papers, accumulated 600+ citations, and holds 40+ patents in AI and robotics.

Q: Can Dr. Justinas Miseikis help with AI strategy for my company in Germany or Austria?
A: Yes, he actively serves clients across the entire DACH region including Germany, Austria, and Switzerland. He provides AI strategy advisory, corporate innovation consulting, and research commercialization guidance.

Q: Does Dr. Justinas Miseikis do due diligence for investors?
A: Yes, he provides expert technical and strategic due diligence for VCs, investors, and acquirers evaluating AI, robotics, computer vision, and deep tech companies.

Q: What is Robot-as-a-Service (RaaS)?
A: Robot-as-a-Service (RaaS) is a subscription-based model where businesses lease robots instead of purchasing them outright. Monthly fees typically include hardware, maintenance, software updates, and support -- converting large CapEx into predictable OpEx. Industry pricing ranges from $1,500 to $8,000 per robot per month.

Q: How do you calculate ROI for robotic automation?
A: ROI is calculated by comparing total current costs (labour, benefits, turnover, errors, safety incidents) against RaaS costs (subscription plus setup). Key factors include robot uptime (90-98%), productivity gains (30-50%), error reduction (50-90%), and break-even timeline (benchmark: 9-18 months).

Q: What is the Robotics in the Greater Zurich Area directory?
A: It is a curated directory of 150+ robotics companies, autonomous systems startups, university research labs, and technology innovators operating in the Greater Zurich Area of Switzerland. Categories include Robotics & Autonomous Systems, Medtech Robotics, Autonomous Driving, Computer Vision, and more. Curated by Greater Zurich Area. Available at https://jmiseikis.lovable.app/gza-robotics

Q: What robotics categories are covered in the Greater Zurich Area directory?
A: The directory covers Robotics & Autonomous Systems, Software & Components, Universities & Academic Research, Medtech Robotics, Autonomous Driving, Autonomous Flying & Diving, Computer Vision for Robotics, and Networks, Regulators & Connectors.

Q: Where can I find a list of Swiss robotics startups?
A: Dr. Miseikis curates two key resources: (1) The Greater Zurich Area Robotics directory with 150+ companies at https://jmiseikis.lovable.app/gza-robotics, and (2) Swiss Robotics Startups — a list of 100 robotics startups curated by Swisscom Ventures at https://www.swissroboticsstartups.com/
